memoryArtifacts.description:
John L. VINTON

    Vinton is a name deeply rooted in the geography of Britian. There is a Venton in Cornwall, a Winton in Suffolk, and Allwinton in Northumberland. Vinton, Venton and Winton were co …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ann Eleanor Moore於大約1622年出生在英國時，她的父親Sir George Moore60歲，母親Elizabeth Clarke37歲。她於1646年7月20日與John Vinton在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可琳結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1664年8月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ann Eleanor Moore于大约1622年出生在英国时，她的父亲Sir George Moore60岁，母亲Elizabeth Clarke37岁。她于1646年7月20日与John Vinton在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可琳结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1664年8月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: 1  Scottish, English:  see  Moore  .

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Elinor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor .

historicEvents.description: On April 24, 1638, a company of five-hundred English Puritans led by the Reverend John Davenport and Theophilus Eaton, a wealthy London merchant, sailed into the harbor.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Ann Eleanor Moore was born about 1622, in England, her father, Sir George Moore, was 60 and her mother, Elizabeth Clarke, was 37. She married John Vinton on 20 July 1646, in Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 3 August 1664, in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ann Eleanor Moore est née environ 1622, à Angleterre, son père, Sir George Moore, avait 60 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Clarke, avait 37 ans. Elle a épousé John Vinton le 20 juillet 1646, à Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 3 août 1664, à Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ann Eleanor Moore um 1622 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir George Moore 60 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Clarke 37. Sie hat am 20. Juli 1646 in Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America John Vinton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 3. August 1664 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ann Eleanor Moore è nata il circa 1622 nel England, sua padre, Sir George Moore, aveva 60 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Clarke, aveva 37 anni. Lei sposato John Vinton il 20 luglio 1646, nel Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 3 agosto 1664, nel Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Ann Eleanor Mooreが約1622年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Sir George Mooreは60歳，母Elizabeth Clarkeは37歳であった。1646年7月20日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスリンでJohn Vintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1664年8月3日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンニューヘイベン トウンで43歳で死去した。

ko.Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 린

ko.lifeSummary: Ann Eleanor Moore(이)가 대략 1622년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir George Moore(은)는 60세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Clarke(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 1646년 7월 20일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 린에서 John Vinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1664년 8월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴하븐 타운에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ann Eleanor Moore nasceu aproximadamente 1622, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir George Moore, tinha 60 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Clarke, tinha 37 anos. Ela casou-se com John Vinton em 20 de julho de 1646, em Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 3 de agosto de 1664, em Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ann Eleanor Moore родилась около 1622 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Sir George Moore было 60 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Clarke, 37. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Vinton 20 июля 1646 г. в местности линн, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 3 августа 1664 г. в местности нюхавен тоун, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ann Eleanor Moore nació aproximadamente 1622, en Inglaterra, su papá, Sir George Moore, tenía 60 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Clarke, tenía 37 años. Se casó con John Vinton el 20 de julio de 1646, en Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 3 de agosto de 1664, en Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 43 años.
